items.title: Commit and review locks
items.prefer: Commit it and review lock diffs in PRs.
items.why: Makes dependency changes visible and reproducible.
items.example: A PR that bumps `pydantic` updates `requirements.lock` with the new pin + hash; reviewers see exactly which transitive packages changed and can ask why.

items.title: Generate, don't edit
items.why: Keeps the lock a consistent solver output.
items.example: Want to bump `urllib3`? Edit `requirements.in` (or `pyproject.toml`) → run `pip-compile` / `poetry lock` / `uv lock` → commit both files. The resolver may need to move other packages to stay consistent.

items.title: Pin with hashes
items.example:
```
httpx==0.27.2 \
    --hash=sha256:abc... \
    --hash=sha256:def...
```
`pip install --require-hashes` refuses to install if the downloaded bytes don't match.

items.title: Enforce the lock in CI
items.prefer: Use lock-respecting/--deploy installs that fail on a stale lock.
items.why: Guarantees CI matches the recorded set.
items.example:
```yaml
- run: poetry install --no-update   # poetry
- run: pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
- run: uv sync --frozen             # uv
```
